workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Give me the manager view on this Iron Creek Mining escalation email. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-02-02
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Kevin (Iron Creek Mining)

[00:01:30] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into executive brief.

[00:05:00] Kevin (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:07:00] Kevin (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:10:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:15:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Kevin (Iron Creek Mining): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Kevin (Iron Creek Mining): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:23:00] Kevin (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-P trap (Coaching-notes output isn't first-class artifact in current skill set); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-P failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-P failure mode (Coaching-notes output isn't first-class artifact in current skill set)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-P -- Coaching-notes output isn't first-class artifact in current skill set
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
